title: Interpret Tote Presence Colors And Distinguish Stations On The Container Selection Screen
summary: Use the Container Selection screen color and label conventions from the training source to determine whether a visible location shows a tote present, an empty rack, or a station. The source states that dark blue indicates a tote is present, light blue indicates an empty rack, and items without those blue indicators are stations with associated IDs.
when_to_use: Use this reference when viewing the Container Selection screen and you need to interpret whether a displayed location contains a tote, is empty, or is a station based on the documented color and label appearance.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to infer any color meaning beyond tote present, empty rack, and station identification stated in the source.
Do not use this runbook as authority for operational actions such as removing items, changing system state, or performing recovery actions.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Container Selection screen and interpret location status using the documented color and label conventions from the training source.

safety_notes: Limit interpretation to the meanings explicitly stated in the source.
Escalate if the observed color or label pattern does not match the documented dark blue, light blue, or station description.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Container Selection screen

access_required: Access to the Container Selection screen
system_preconditions: The user is viewing the Container Selection screen.
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1, segment training_video_day1_segment_0039

visual_references.description: Training frame showing the Container Selection view with dark blue and light blue rack indicators and non-blue labeled items described as stations.

steps.title: View the Container Selection indicators
steps.purpose: Locate the visual indicators on the Container Selection screen before interpreting status.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Container Selection screen and look at the rack or container indicators shown on the display.
steps.expected_result: The relevant rack, container, or labeled screen items are visible for interpretation.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen shows the location indicators clearly enough to compare dark blue, light blue, and non-blue labeled items.
steps.failure_condition: The indicators are not visible or cannot be visually distinguished on the screen.

actions.label: View container indicators
actions.exact_action: View the rack or container indicators on the Container Selection screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Dark blue and light blue indicators and examples of items without those indicators on the Container Selection screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Container Selection screen is not available.
The visible indicators do not match the documented screen example closely enough to interpret.

steps.title: Check whether the location is dark blue or light blue
steps.purpose: Identify whether the visible location matches one of the documented occupancy colors.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the selected or visible location is marked dark blue or light blue.
steps.expected_result: The location is identified as either dark blue, light blue, or not one of those blue indicators.
steps.healthy_condition: The location color can be matched to one of the documented blue states or recognized as not matching them.
steps.failure_condition: The color cannot be determined or appears inconsistent with the documented examples.

actions.label: Observe location color
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the selected or visible location is marked dark blue or light blue.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The blue color states used on the Container Selection screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed color does not clearly appear dark blue or light blue.
The observed color pattern does not match the documented training description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Dark blue indicates the presence of a tote, while light blue represents empty rack; containers without those blue indicators are described as stations.

steps.title: Interpret blue occupancy colors
steps.purpose: Apply the documented meaning of the two blue occupancy states.
steps.instruction: Interpret dark blue as indicating the presence of a tote and light blue as representing an empty rack.
steps.expected_result: The viewed location is correctly classified as tote present or empty rack when one of the documented blue colors is shown.
steps.healthy_condition: Dark blue is treated only as tote present and light blue is treated only as empty rack.
steps.failure_condition: A blue state is interpreted with a meaning not supported by the source.

actions.label: Apply color meaning
actions.exact_action: Interpret dark blue as indicating the presence of a tote and light blue as representing an empty rack.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Examples of dark blue and light blue occupancy indicators.

stop_or_escalate_if: The screen appears to use a different meaning than the documented dark blue or light blue definitions.
Additional color meanings seem necessary to interpret the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Dark blue indicates the presence of a tote, while light blue represents empty rack; containers without those blue indicators are described as stations.

source_evidence.title: Container color indicators on the screen
source_evidence.summary: The slide uses color coding to indicate whether a rack location contains a tote.

steps.title: Treat non-blue labeled items as stations
steps.purpose: Distinguish stations from standard tote occupancy locations.
steps.instruction: If an item does not have the blue or dark blue tote indicators, compare it to the training note that such items are stations rather than standard tote locations.
steps.expected_result: Items without the documented blue occupancy indicators are recognized as stations.
steps.healthy_condition: Non-blue labeled items are distinguished from tote-present and empty-rack locations.
steps.failure_condition: A non-blue labeled item is incorrectly treated as a standard tote occupancy location.

actions.label: Identify station items
actions.exact_action: If an item does not have the blue or dark blue tote indicators, compare it to the training note that such items are stations rather than standard tote locations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Items on the screen that do not use the blue occupancy indicators.

stop_or_escalate_if: A non-blue item cannot be confidently distinguished from a tote location.
The label pattern does not match the documented station description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Items without the blue or dark blue indicators are described as stations; they may look like labels physically but have more properties and associated IDs.

source_evidence.title: Station labels are distinct from tote indicators and have associated IDs
source_evidence.summary: The training distinguishes certain labeled screen elements as stations rather than standard tote locations and notes that these stations have associated IDs.

steps.title: Note that stations have associated IDs
steps.purpose: Capture the final distinguishing characteristic stated for stations in the source.
steps.instruction: When a station is identified, note that the training says stations have associated IDs.
steps.expected_result: The user recognizes that station items are associated with IDs.
steps.healthy_condition: A station is understood as a distinct labeled item with an associated ID.
steps.failure_condition: The user assumes a station is only a visual label without the associated ID characteristic described in the source.

actions.label: Note station IDs
actions.exact_action: When a station is identified, note that the training says stations have associated IDs.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Station-like labeled items distinguished from blue occupancy indicators.

stop_or_escalate_if: A station appears without the expected associated ID information described in the source.
The screen element cannot be reconciled with the training description of a station.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Items without the blue or dark blue indicators are described as stations; they may look like labels physically but have more properties and associated IDs.

success_criteria: The user can distinguish dark blue locations as tote present.
The user can distinguish light blue locations as empty rack.
The user can distinguish non-blue labeled items as stations.
The user understands that stations have associated IDs.
healthy_conditions: Dark blue is interpreted as tote present.
Light blue is interpreted as empty rack.
Items without the documented blue indicators are interpreted as stations.
Interpretation remains limited to meanings explicitly stated in the source.
failure_conditions: The observed color or label pattern does not match the documented dark blue, light blue, or station description.
Additional status meanings are inferred from colors without source support.
A station is confused with a standard tote occupancy location.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed color or label pattern does not match the documented dark blue, light blue, or station description.
Escalate if the screen cannot be interpreted using only the source-backed meanings in this runbook.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Container Selection screen example showing dark blue, light blue, and non-blue station items.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Dark blue indicates the presence of a tote, while light blue represents empty rack; containers without those blue indicators are described as stations.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide a formal role boundary beyond operator-level use.
The source packet does not provide a time estimate.
The source packet does not provide explicit do-not-use cases beyond limiting interpretation to the documented meanings.
The source packet does not provide additional screenshots specifically isolating station IDs.
